signals.title: etalytics — AI Energy Optimisation for Industrial Clients
signals.reasoning: etalytics is a strong thesis fit: a German deep-tech spinout from TU Darmstadt commercialising AI-driven energy optimisation backed by Microsoft M12. Their etaONE product uses digital twins to cut power consumption by up to 50% for enterprise clients including Equinix and Volkswagen — clear technical moat with measurable ROI at Series A stage.
thesis_alignment: DACH founding team (Darmstadt, Germany)
AI/ML core technology applied to industrial infrastructure
Series A stage — €16M raised (Alstin Capital + Microsoft M12)
Named enterprise clients with measurable ROI (Equinix, Volkswagen)
Sustainability & Green Tech — strong policy and regulatory tailwind
red_flags: Niche ICP limits scaling speed — data centres and heavy industry only
signals.risk: Customer concentration in data centres; revenue exposed if hyperscalers build in-house energy optimisation.
signals.previous_rounds: Series A €16M — Alstin Capital + Microsoft M12 (2024)

signals.title: finmid — Embedded B2B Lending Infrastructure for Platforms
signals.reasoning: finmid is a textbook DACH Series A: two ex-N26 founders building embedded lending infrastructure for B2B platforms across 20+ European markets. Strong traction with Wolt, Delivery Hero, and Bolt; customers grow sales 45% in six months. €35M Series A led by Blossom Capital and Earlybird confirms institutional conviction.
thesis_alignment: German founding team with pan-European product (20+ countries)
Series A stage — €35M raised (Blossom Capital + Earlybird + N26's Max Tayenthal)
B2B infrastructure model — not consumer
Clear revenue proxy: platform customers grow 45% in 6 months
Experienced operators (ex-N26 leadership)
red_flags: Embedded finance is increasingly competitive — Stripe and Adyen expanding
signals.risk: Platform dependency risk — losing one large partner such as Wolt could significantly impact revenue.
signals.previous_rounds: Pre-seed €2M → Seed €10M → Series A €35M (Blossom Capital + Earlybird, 2024)

signals.title: Authologic — Digital Identity Verification Hub for KYC/AML
signals.reasoning: Authologic is a compelling CEE Series A: a Warsaw-founded identity orchestration platform backed by YC and OpenOcean serving 80+ clients including Santander and eToro. The aggregator model across fragmented EU e-ID standards is defensible, and eIDAS 2.0 and EU AML directives provide strong regulatory tailwinds.
thesis_alignment: CEE founding team (Warsaw, Poland) — high-value geography for this fund
Series A stage — $8.2M (OpenOcean + Y Combinator, 2024)
B2B compliance infrastructure — recurring, sticky revenue
Named enterprise clients: Santander, eToro, Superbet
Regulatory tailwind: eIDAS 2.0 and EU 6th AML Directive
red_flags: Crowded space — Onfido, Veriff, Sumsub all competing on similar positioning
signals.risk: Commoditisation risk as e-ID verification standardises across EU; moat must come from orchestration depth, not just coverage.

signals.title: Remi Health — Diagnostics-as-a-Service API Platform
signals.reasoning: Remi Health is building API-first diagnostic infrastructure for European healthcare — a defensible niche given fragmented EU health systems. Processing 1M+ tests with a €5M seed and MSD backing shows early validation. Currently Seed stage so slightly pre-Series A for this thesis, but worth tracking for the next round.
thesis_alignment: German founding team (Berlin/Potsdam)
API-first infrastructure model — true platform play
Healthcare payer partnerships provide distribution moat
1M+ diagnostics processed — meaningful early traction
red_flags: Seed stage — likely 12–18 months pre-Series A
Multi-country healthcare regulation adds expansion complexity
signals.risk: Distribution dependent on payer relationships; losing key contracts could stall growth significantly.
signals.previous_rounds: Seed €5M — IBB Ventures + Catalpa Ventures + MSD (2023)

signals.title: PeopleForce — HR Platform Built for CEE and Emerging Markets
signals.reasoning: PeopleForce is a Ukrainian-founded B2B SaaS HR platform showing remarkable resilience — 10x revenue growth since seed despite geopolitical headwinds, now managing 160K+ employees across 1,500 clients. CEE origin and expansion into Poland positions it for EU market entry. Pre-Series A stage and crowded HR tech market are watch items.
thesis_alignment: CEE founding team (Ukraine) — aligns with fund's geography focus
B2B SaaS with clear ARR model — 1,500+ clients, 160K employees managed
10x revenue growth since seed — exceptional traction trajectory
Expanding into Poland (EU) — demonstrating European ambition
red_flags: HR tech is highly competitive — Personio, BambooHR dominate
Pre-Series A — slightly early for thesis
signals.risk: Geopolitical risk from Ukraine HQ may complicate enterprise sales cycles and EU regulatory compliance for prospective customers.
signals.previous_rounds: Pre-Series A $5.4M — Pracuj Ventures + u.ventures + Sturgeon Capital (2025)

signals.title: Helsing — AI Software for European Defence
signals.reasoning: Helsing is arguably Europe's most consequential defence AI company — German-founded, backed by Spotify's Daniel Ek, valued at €12B with proprietary drone systems deployed in Ukraine. Technically exceptional with a clear AI moat. However at €1.37B raised and €12B valuation it is firmly beyond Series A territory; relevant here as a sector benchmark.
thesis_alignment: German founding team (Munich) — strong DACH representation
AI/ML at core — real-time multi-sensor data processing
Deployed government revenue (Ukrainian MoD)
Deep technical moat — proprietary hardware + AI software stack
red_flags: €12B valuation — far beyond Series A investment stage
Defence sector requires specialist LP base and compliance infrastructure
signals.risk: Revenue tied to active conflict and government procurement cycles; both are inherently unpredictable.
signals.previous_rounds: Series A €100M (2021) → Series B €209M (General Catalyst, 2023) → Series C €450M (2024) → €600M (Prima Materia, 2025 — valued at €12B)
